markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Generate wiki/topic pages from indexed sources with citations, stale markers, and regeneration controls. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: WikiPageProjectionCompiler leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: WikiPageProjectionCompiler is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: Wiki/topic-page projection compiler over LoomBlock authority, on the existing knowledge_wiki_projections store (NEVER authority; no authority FK targets it). No parallel projection infra.
files_changed: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs - impls + render_loom_wiki_markdown (deterministic, per-block citations) + loom_wiki_staleness_hash (sha256 over source block identity+content+updated_at) + loom_wiki_projection_from_knowledge; reuses KnowledgeStore upsert/get/mark_rebuilt projection methods

evidence.result: ok. 5 passed; 0 failed (real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL) [shared MT-184/185/187 suite]
tests: wiki_projection_compiles_with_citations_and_staleness - source_block_ids citations recorded; content cites blocks; fresh after compile; editing a source block -> stale; regenerate -> fresh + reflects rename
wiki_apis_fail_closed - compile citing a missing block fails closed (no dangling citation); get missing projection errors

evidence: Status flip note (2026-06-12T06:25:27.785Z): Loom engine group backend-complete per delegate report - real-PG proven (isolated schemas, full migration chain), per-MT adversarial review recorded in this file. SQLite removed/unreachable (orphans git-rm'd, sqlx has no sqlite feature, source regression guard added). Markdown-vault-never-authority proven via projection-delete byte-identity negative test. Stable /workspaces/:ws/loom/* HTTP contracts documented for the Notes UI. Implementation commit(s): 043cf8c8.

findings: DANGLING CITATION (handled+tested): compile loads every source block and fails closed on a missing one, so source_records citations are always valid.
DETERMINISM (handled): render_loom_wiki_markdown + loom_wiki_staleness_hash are pure functions of (title, ordered blocks); same authority renders byte-identically.
STALENESS (handled+tested): the hash folds block id+title+content_type+full_text_index+updated_at; any source edit (or a since-deleted source) flips stale.
PROJECTION-NEVER-AUTHORITY (handled+tested in MT-187 negative test): the projection is a knowledge_wiki_projections row with no authority FK into it.
WORKSPACE SCOPING (clear): get/delete filter on workspace_id (the underlying store is keyed by projection_id; the Loom wrapper enforces workspace ownership).
fixes: No code change required; findings handled-by-design and tested.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-184 requires a WikiPageProjectionCompiler that generates wiki/topic pages from indexed LoomBlock sources with per-block citations, stale markers, and regeneration controls, all as PostgreSQL/EventLedger-backed projections (never authority). The committed code (commit 043cf8c8) implements this fully across storage, API, and real-PostgreSQL tests, and aligns with the current Master Spec's projection-as-cache / projection-never-authority law.
findings: postgres.rs:6169 compile_loom_wiki_projection loads each source block via get_loom_block and FAILS CLOSED on a missing block (no dangling citations) before rendering + hashing + upserting into the pre-existing knowledge_wiki_projections store.

postgres.rs:1751 render_loom_wiki_markdown is a pure deterministic function emitting per-block 'cite: loom_block:<id>' citations; postgres.rs:1796 loom_wiki_staleness_hash folds block id+title+content_type+full_text_index+updated_at over sha256, so any source edit or deletion flips staleness.

postgres.rs:6224 loom_wiki_projection_is_stale recomputes the hash over CURRENT source blocks and returns true if a source block was since-deleted (NotFound -> stale); regenerate_loom_wiki_projection (6245) re-renders from current authority and reflects renames.

postgres.rs:6276 delete_loom_wiki_projection mutates NO authority record; tests/loom_wiki_boundary_tests.rs proves block authority is BYTE-IDENTICAL (block_fingerprint before==after) and the knowledge bridge (entity_id, index_event_id) is unchanged after projection delete.

tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs runs against REAL Handshake-managed PostgreSQL (managed_postgres::ManagedPostgres::ensure_running, isolated per-test schemas, full migration chain) with NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback; binaries-absent path SKIPs, every other failure panics so a broken cluster cannot look green.
api/loom.rs:130-151 wires real routes POST /loom/wiki, GET/DELETE /loom/wiki/:id, /regenerate, /stale to the storage methods; no placeholder handlers.

Spec alignment: master-spec-v02.194 module 10 treats projections/markdown mirrors as cache 'safe to delete and regenerate' (line 7343) and 'never treated as authority' (line 8489); the implementation matches this projection-never-authority law.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): adversarial_code_review is implementer self-review and the evidence test command was not re-run by me (host-load constraint); proof realism judged by reading test + harness code, which is genuine.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
